Title: Chrome extension installation must be restricted
Why It Matters: A new high-severity control was added, so it should be triaged before lower-risk backlog work.
Check Summary: Review Chrome enterprise extension policy and confirm unapproved extension installation is blocked or allowlisted.
Fix Summary: Configure Chrome ExtensionInstallBlocklist and ExtensionInstallAllowlist policies through GPO or registry.
Evidence Needed: Screenshot or export of the relevant setting; GPO, registry, or Local Security Policy export; Date/time of validation; System or asset name; Reviewer notes

Title: Deprecated Chrome cleanup policy must be reviewed
Why It Matters: The control was removed, so downstream tickets, evidence requests, or mappings may need cleanup.
Check Summary: Review legacy Chrome cleanup policy settings.
Fix Summary: Remove or disable legacy Chrome cleanup configuration.
Evidence Needed: Screenshot or export of the relevant setting; Date/time of validation; System or asset name; Reviewer notes

Title: Chrome Safe Browsing enhanced protection must be enabled
Tags: IAM; Endpoint Security; GPO; Registry; Browser Security
Why It Matters: The severity increased to high and the remediation text changed, so old tickets or implementation notes should not be reused without review.
Check Summary: Review Chrome enterprise policy in GPO or registry and export evidence showing SafeBrowsingProtectionLevel is set to enhanced protection.
Fix Summary: Configure Chrome Safe Browsing enhanced protection through Group Policy or the registry policy path and document the applied baseline.
Evidence Needed: Screenshot or export of the relevant setting; GPO, registry, or Local Security Policy export; Date/time of validation; System or asset name; Reviewer notes

Title: Chrome password manager must be disabled
Tags: Password Policy; GPO; Registry; Browser Security
Why It Matters: The check procedure changed enough that evidence requests or validation steps may need to be refreshed.
Check Summary: Review Chrome enterprise policy in GPO or registry and export evidence showing PasswordManagerEnabled is disabled for managed users.
Fix Summary: Disable Chrome Password Manager using enterprise policy.
Evidence Needed: Screenshot or export of the relevant setting; GPO, registry, or Local Security Policy export; Date/time of validation; System or asset name; Reviewer notes
